svg.text: YEMENITE HEBREW
HISTORY — JEWS OF ARABIA
JEWISH SETTLEMENT IN ARABIA
Jews in Yemen: tradition traces to time of King Solomon
Archaeological evidence: Jewish presence from at least 2nd century BCE
Yemen: significant Jewish community sustained for ~2000 years
Pre-Islamic Arabia: Jews in Medina, Khaybar, Najran
DHU NUWAS: JEWISH KING OF HIMYAR
Himyarite Kingdom (modern Yemen): converted to Judaism ~380 CE
Dhu Nuwas (r. ~517-525 CE): last Jewish king of Arabia
Massacred Christian community of Najran (possibly ~525 CE)
Ethiopian Christian invasion ended Himyarite kingdom; Islam followed
YEMENITE JEWRY UNDER ISLAM
Dhimmi status: protected minority but restricted; wore distinctive clothing
Mawza Exile (1679): expelled from cities to hot coastal lowlands
Despite hardship: preserved most complete Hebrew oral tradition
ARABIC AND YEMENITE JEWS · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
OPERATION MAGIC CARPET (1949-1950)
~50,000 Yemenite Jews airlifted to Israel over 16 months
Most had never seen an airplane; saw it as fulfillment of Isaiah 40:31
"They shall mount up with wings as eagles..."
Brought: Torah scrolls, manuscripts, and 2000 years of Hebrew tradition
ARABIC AS YEMENITE JEWISH LANGUAGE
For 1300 years: Yemenite Jews spoke Judeo-Yemeni Arabic daily
Judeo-Yemeni: Arabic dialect with Hebrew/Aramaic loanwords
Hebrew prayers + Arabic daily speech: bilingual life for generations
YEMENITE HEBREW TODAY
Yemenite Jews in Israel: Hebrew/Arabic bilingualism continued
Yemenite liturgy: preserved in Israeli synagogues; recorded 20th century
Most conservative Hebrew phonology: now serves as reference for scholars
Unique window into Proto-Semitic: both languages at their most ancient
PLATE 11d ALT · YEMENITE HEBREW / ARABIC · HISTORY
